failure_modes.text: Motivated System 2 recruits deliberate reasoning to construct justifications for preferences already generated by System 1, producing outputs that look deliberative but are not.
failure_modes.mitigation: Install a motivated-reasoning check: after constructing a deliberate argument, ask whether the conclusion was already preferred before the analysis began — if so, run the analysis against the opposite hypothesis.

failure_modes.text: Cognitive depletion windows reduce System 2 capacity across heavy decision sessions, making late-day or end-of-review decisions structurally disadvantaged.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect late-session decisions: schedule the highest-stakes judgments early in the day or after breaks, and flag any critical call made under cognitive fatigue for re-review.

failure_modes.text: Pseudo-deliberation performs the motions of System 2 engagement — the memo, the meeting, the rubric — without genuinely updating on contrary evidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test for genuine updating: after any deliberative process, identify what changed in the team's position — if nothing changed, the deliberation was performative.

premortem_questions.text: If we had no prior position on this, which option would this analysis actually support — or are we constructing reasons for a conclusion we've already reached?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Motivated System 2: System 2 as advocate rather than auditor

premortem_questions.text: Are we making this decision now because it is the right time for deliberate analysis, or because the meeting happens to end here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Depletion windows: consequential decisions made late in the day are structurally disadvantaged

premortem_questions.text: Has anyone in this process genuinely identified a reason to change the recommended conclusion — or has every challenge been addressed as an obstacle to resolve?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Pseudo-deliberation: process is theater; System 1 consensus forms first

heuristics.text: Separate the generate phase from the evaluate phase — collect options and independent ratings before group discussion to prevent early System 1 consensus from making System 2 review performative.
heuristics.source_quote: Require anonymous independent ratings before group discussion

heuristics.text: Schedule high-stakes decisions early in the session, when System 2 capacity is highest — not at the end of an exhausting review agenda.
heuristics.source_quote: Schedule high-stakes decisions early in the day, after adequate rest and food

heuristics.text: Externalize the reasoning chain in writing before the group meeting — unwritten assumptions are invisible to System 2 audit and to other participants.
heuristics.source_quote: Externalize the Reasoning: writing activates System 2 and makes its outputs inspectable

curation_notes.summary: Wave 2 for System 2 focuses on the conditions under which deliberate reasoning fails to function as advertised — motivated justification, depletion, and performative deliberation — with process-level heuristics for genuine engagement.
